description_long: This is a classic, hand-thrown fragmentation grenade with a dark green, segmented metal body designed for grip and fragmentation upon detonation. The upper section features a prominent safety lever (spoon) and a metal pull ring attached to a visible safety pin, both rendered in a metallic gray. The body is covered in raised, square-patterned segments, which are intended to enhance grip and create shrapnel upon explosion. The surface finish is slightly worn, with visible scuff marks and a matte sheen, suggesting it has seen some handling. The overall form is compact and ovoid, with the safety lever running along one side and the pin mechanism clearly visible from the front. No visible warning labels or digital components are present, and the object is visually consistent with a traditional military grenade.
description: A compact, dark green, segmented hand grenade with a metal safety lever and pull ring, featuring a rugged, matte finish and classic fragmentation design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the safety lever, pull ring, and pin mechanism clearly, with the segmented body and matte green finish prominent.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the width of the safety lever running along the top and side, with the pull ring partially visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the safety lever and the segmented body, with the pull ring and pin less visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the safety lever's edge and the full curvature of the grenade body, with the pin mechanism barely visible.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is initiated by pulling the safety pin and releasing the safety lever, which triggers an internal timed fuse.
